etymology.description: semantic 寸(寸) (cùn, "hand") + phonetic 道(道) (dào, "path") -> to lead someone
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 道(道) (dào, "path") as the phonetic element with 寸(寸) (cùn, "hand") as the semantic element, suggesting the concept of using one's hand to guide along a path. This combination reflects the meaning of guiding or leading someone.
etymology.simplification: The traditional character 導(導) was simplified to 导 by replacing 道(道) with a simpler top radical arrangement, retaining the core concept.

examples.chinese: 老师正在指导学生们(老師正在指導學生們)。
examples.english: The teacher is guiding the students.

examples.chinese: 导游会带我们参观城市(導游會帶我們參觀城市)。
examples.pinyin: Dǎoyóu huì dài wǒmen cānguān chéngshì.
examples.english: The guide will take us to tour the city.

examples.chinese: 他负责导演这部新电影(他負責導演這部新電影)。
examples.pinyin: Tā fùzé dǎoyǎn zhè bù xīn diànyǐng.
examples.english: He is responsible for directing this new movie.
